user_query:
I’m preparing a short foreword for a restoration package and want to ground my reflections in how my work was received at the time. Please do two things using the files in input/:

1) Extract a structured summary of how my performance was described in these period reviews:
   - input/reviews/review_1949_desert_star.html
   - input/reviews/review_1953_stagecoach.md
   - input/reviews/review_1957_serenade.txt

   For each file, find the first sentence that contains my name exactly as “Elliot Grant” and treat that as the actor_sentence. If a file has no such sentence, skip it. From each file, also extract: film, outlet, reviewer, date (YYYY-MM-DD), and review_title (title of the review). Save one row per included file to output/review_summary.csv with these columns in this exact order:
   source_file, film, outlet, reviewer, date, review_title, actor_sentence, tone

   Determine tone for each actor_sentence by keyword matching (case-insensitive substring match on the whole sentence) using the following lexicons:
   - Positive keywords: ["brilliant", "mesmerizing", "superb", "luminous", "deft", "poignant", "commanding", "remarkable", "strong", "riveting"]
   - Negative keywords: ["wooden", "flat", "weak", "uneven", "dull", "miscast", "drag", "plodding", "overdone", "stiff", "tired"]
   Tone rules:
   - positive if the sentence contains at least one positive keyword and no negative keywords;
   - negative if it contains at least one negative keyword and no positive keywords;
   - mixed if it contains both positive and negative keywords, or contains neither list.

2) Edit my draft foreword at input/letters/foreword_draft.md by replacing the single line that reads [[INSERT CRITIQUE SUMMARY]] with a new paragraph that:
   - States how many reviews contained an actor_sentence and reports the count of positive, mixed, and negative tones based on output/review_summary.csv;
   - Includes two short quotations: (a) the most positive actor_sentence and (b) the most negative actor_sentence, each followed by the outlet and year in parentheses. If there are multiple candidates within a tone, choose the one with the earliest date. If a tone is unavailable, fall back to a mixed sentence for that slot; if none exists, omit that quote entirely;
   - Keeps everything else in the document unchanged.

Save the revised draft as output/foreword_revised.md. The task is complete when both output/review_summary.csv and output/foreword_revised.md are created and consistent with the inputs and the rules above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
